source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline (Update): Earwax (Cerumen Impaction)
source.population: People older than 6 months with cerumen impaction, excluding specified ear-canal, radiation, and prior ear-surgery contexts.
source.setting: Any setting in which cerumen impaction is identified, monitored, or managed.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected cerumen symptoms, otoscopy, modifying factors, post-treatment assessment, and qualified referral documentation. Removal procedures, drops, irrigation, treatment, hearing-aid selection, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Scope, definition, and symptoms of cerumen impaction
exact_location.locator: Full guideline supporting text and Key Action Statement 2A; official full-text lines 183–217 and 417–475

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.

exact_location.locator: Full guideline supporting text and Key Action Statement 2A; official full-text lines 183–217 and 417–475

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.

assessment.text: Supports diagnosis only when clinician-observed cerumen is symptomatic or prevents assessment, plus history or examination of management-modifying factors.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician-observed impaction and management-modifying history or examination; no treatment item is mapped.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline (Update): Earwax (Cerumen Impaction)
source.population: People older than 6 months with cerumen impaction, excluding specified ear-canal, radiation, and prior ear-surgery contexts.
source.setting: Any setting in which cerumen impaction is identified, monitored, or managed.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected cerumen symptoms, otoscopy, modifying factors, post-treatment assessment, and qualified referral documentation. Removal procedures, drops, irrigation, treatment, hearing-aid selection, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Diagnosis and modifying factors
exact_location.locator: Key Action Statements 2A–2B; official full-text lines 302–305 and 417–528

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports diagnosis only when clinician-observed cerumen is symptomatic or prevents assessment, plus history or examination of management-modifying factors.

assessment.text: Supports documenting resolution after clinician treatment, considering alternate causes when symptoms persist after resolution, and clinician referral after unsuccessful initial management without mapping a procedure or treatment instruction.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped clinician referral after unsuccessful management; broad or fixed follow-up fields are not mapped.

exact_location.heading: Outcomes assessment and referral
exact_location.locator: Key Action Statements 9–10; official full-text lines 315–316 and 1137 onward

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting resolution after clinician treatment, considering alternate causes when symptoms persist after resolution, and clinician referral after unsuccessful initial management without mapping a procedure or treatment instruction.

assessment.text: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped sudden or rapid hearing change and neurological warning fields in adults.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
source.setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult hearing-difficulty assessment, red-flag referral, otoscopy, audiology, and hearing-support documentation; partial for children and UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — initial, sudden, and urgent assessment
exact_location.locator: lines 43–71

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

assessment.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped tinnitus, vertigo, pain, discharge, and abnormal-ear context; overlapping fields use the cerumen source.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
source.setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult hearing-difficulty assessment, red-flag referral, otoscopy, audiology, and hearing-support documentation; partial for children and UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.3.2 — additional symptoms, ear findings, and referral
exact_location.locator: lines 72–167

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

assessment.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped hearing-screen, audiology, hearing-aid discussion, questions, and clinician-arranged audiology documentation in adults.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline (Update): Earwax (Cerumen Impaction)
source.population: People older than 6 months with cerumen impaction, excluding specified ear-canal, radiation, and prior ear-surgery contexts.
source.setting: Any setting in which cerumen impaction is identified, monitored, or managed.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected cerumen symptoms, otoscopy, modifying factors, post-treatment assessment, and qualified referral documentation. Removal procedures, drops, irrigation, treatment, hearing-aid selection, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
